body.text: An array holds multiple values under a single variable name. Each value has a position number called an index. Arrays always start counting at zero — the first item is at index 0, not index 1.

body.desc: A list of two text values stored in one variable
Get the item at index 0 — the first item
How many items are in the array

body.label: Zero indexing
body.text: Arrays start at index 0, not 1. A 4-item array has indices 0, 1, 2, 3. Accessing index 4 returns undefined.

exercises.title: Read an array
tasks: Before running: write down scores[0], scores[5], scores[6], and scores.length.
Type it in the editor and use console.log to check each answer.
Now push a new number onto the array. What is scores.length now? What index is the new item at?
exercises.hint: The last valid index is always array.length - 1. Anything beyond that is undefined.

checks.label: console logs the value at index 0 (which is 4)

exercises.title: Build and modify an array
tasks: Create an array called favorites with four items — anything you like.
Log the first and last items using their indexes.
Push two more items onto the array.
Remove the item at index 1 using splice.
Log the full array after each change to watch it update.
exercises.hint: To log the whole array: console.log(favorites). To remove at index 1: favorites.splice(1, 1).
